title: Macekre of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Macekre of Evangelion?             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jun 17, 2008 1:23 pm I had a surreal idea last night. Here's my question: what would Evangelion be like if it were translated/otherwise rendered by someone like...oh, say, Carl Macek (the guy behind Robotech )? I have a few ideas about this myself, but I can't quite get it into a suitably hilarious shape. So...Evangelion, Carl Macek, take it away.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Jun 17, 2008 2:06 pm SIGINT's nightmare comes to mind.

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Tue Jun 17, 2008 7:17 pm The only thing Macek really did right was the Robotech Macross Saga, I'd steer clear of anything non-Robotech from him.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Jun 17, 2008 9:23 pm BobBQ wrote: SIGINT's nightmare comes to mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Fri Jun 20, 2008 6:19 pm Do you mean...the stereotypical edited-for-TV dub, or a chimerical mash-up of unrelated series? 'Cause...by eighties TV standards, Robotech wasn't that edited for the usual culprits (for alcohol and smoking), though some stuff in this vein was cut. If it's the first one, Macek is old hat. The kids are demonizing Alfred R. Khan and 4Kids these days.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Jun 22, 2008 9:00 am Incisivis wrote: Do you mean...the stereotypical edited-for-TV dub, or a chimerical mash-up of unrelated series? 'Cause...by eighties TV standards, Robotech wasn't that edited for the usual culprits (for alcohol and smoking), though some stuff in this vein was cut. If it's the first one, Macek is old hat. The kids are demonizing Alfred R. Khan and 4Kids these days.
